question: The scatter plot shows daily screen time (x-axis) and hours of sleep (y-axis), with point size and color also varying. Which statement is correct according to the chart data?

options.A: People with about 5–6 hours of daily screen time tend to have the most sleep, around 9.5–9.8 hours.
options.B: People with about 10–11 hours of daily screen time clearly get the most sleep, above 10 hours.
options.C: People with about 1–2 hours of daily screen time consistently sleep more than all other groups.
options.D: Across all screen-time levels, average sleep time is roughly the same, with no noticeable pattern.

explanation: This scatter plot uses dual encoding: point position shows the relationship, but point size and color also encode the y-variable, making high values overly salient. That can tempt viewers to overstate the strength or form of the relationship.
